message:
feat: migrate compaction worker to externally-injected runtime handles (bd-xdcrh.4.2)

Refactor compaction worker lifecycle management so the runtime handle is
provided by the caller rather than obtained internally, improving testability
and enabling shared runtime ownership across agent subsystems.

AgentSession changes (src/agent.rs):
- Add compaction_runtime and runtime_handle fields for lifecycle management
- Add with_runtime_handle() builder method to accept external runtime injection
- New compaction_runtime_handle() helper: returns injected handle if available,
  falls back to creating a RuntimeBuilder for backward compatibility
- Pass runtime handle to compaction worker's start() call
- Add test: compaction_runtime_handle_creates_fallback_runtime()

CompactionWorker changes (src/compaction_worker.rs):
- Change start() to accept &RuntimeHandle instead of calling Runtime::current_handle()
- Remove Result<()> return type (void)
- Update all 7 existing tests to inject RuntimeHandle via updated helpers
- Rename ready_pending() → ready_pending_with_handle(), parked_pending() →
  parked_pending_with_handle()

Integration points:
- src/main.rs: chain .with_runtime_handle(runtime_handle.clone()) to builder
- src/bin/pi_debug.rs: activate previously unused runtime_handle parameter

Closes bd-xdcrh.4.2 (evaluate compaction and adjacent background workers
for runtime ownership).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.3.3","title":"Add regression coverage for cancellation latency and deadline honoring in looped flows","description":"Add regression coverage for cancellation latency and deadline honoring in the looped flows touched by this workstream. These tests should prove that the checkpoint changes are actually doing something useful and should avoid flaky wall-clock timing whenever a deterministic timer-driver or lab-style harness is available.","design":"Testing guidance:
- Prefer deterministic or semi-deterministic timing control when possible.
- Assert behavior that matters: prompt cancellation observation, bounded deadline honoring, and no broken output or persistence semantics.
- Keep each test tied to the specific loop behavior that motivated the checkpoint.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when each changed looped flow has regression coverage for cancellation or deadline behavior, the tests would fail for the right reason if checkpoint cooperation disappeared, and the suite avoids unnecessary flakiness.","notes":"Use the extension and runtime test patterns already present in this repo as the local reference for preferring deterministic timing semantics over wall-clock sleeps wherever practical.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:23.442266961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T21:01:56.711115172Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T21:01:56.711095084Z","close_reason":"Implemented the approved checkpoint sites in src/tools.rs, src/rpc.rs, and src/agent.rs, documented the deliberate defers in docs/asupersync-leverage-inventory.md, and added focused cancellation/checkpoint regression tests for each changed loop.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","checkpoint","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:23.442266961Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:45.355416557Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.4","title":"Replace only the high-value raw thread islands with runtime-managed blocking work","description":"Evaluate and improve the places where core Pi surfaces still rely on raw threads or bespoke blocking-worker patterns. This is not a blanket replace threads with spawn_blocking effort. Some raw threads, especially around subprocess pipe pumping or other hard-blocking OS interactions, may remain the right choice.","design":"Working rules:
- Classify first, migrate second.
- Record explicit keep or migrate reasoning for each worker island.
- Use runtime-managed blocking work only when it genuinely improves semantics or lifecycle behavior.
- Preserve simple dedicated-thread designs where OS or blocking behavior makes them the clearest correct choice.","acceptance_criteria":"This workstream is complete when the high-value worker islands are evaluated explicitly, approved migrations land with tests and rationale, and deferred raw-thread islands remain by decision rather than neglect.","notes":"Likely focus areas include session JSONL persistence and rewrite paths, compaction or background worker ownership, and the subprocess output pump threads in src/tools.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:24.805434153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:45.677115262Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","blocking-io","persistence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:24.805434153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:45.677024332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4006,"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Some raw threads in this repo may still be the right answer, especially for hard-blocking subprocess pipe reads. This workstream is intentionally framed as evaluation-first so future contributors know the difference between an intentional dedicated-thread design and an unexamined legacy island.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.4.1","title":"Evaluate session JSONL persistence workers for spawn_blocking_io migration","description":"Evaluate session JSONL persistence and rewrite workers to determine whether they should move onto runtime-managed blocking I/O lanes. These paths are strong candidates because they already embody durable file semantics and may benefit from clearer runtime ownership without the complexity of process I/O edge cases.","design":"Decision criteria:
- Would spawn_blocking_io or a nearby runtime-owned primitive preserve or improve durability semantics?
- Would migration make cancellation, shutdown, or quiescence easier to reason about?
- Would migration reduce bespoke thread and channel scaffolding without hiding failure behavior?","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when the relevant session persistence worker sites are cataloged, a clear migrate or defer decision with reasoning is recorded, and any approved migration path is specific enough for implementation without repeating the evaluation.","notes":"Primary target area is src/session.rs, especially the append and rewrite worker islands used for JSONL persistence; preserve file-system correctness guarantees first and runtime elegance second.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:26.445228157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T00:34:59.818148888Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T00:34:59.818127338Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","blocking-io","session"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:45.998994372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:26.445228157Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","title":"Evaluate compaction and adjacent background workers for runtime ownership","description":"Evaluate compaction and adjacent background-worker ownership for possible runtime-managed execution. The key question is whether these workers currently live outside the runtime in a way that meaningfully weakens quiescent shutdown, cancellation composition, or lifecycle reasoning.","design":"Decision criteria:
- What currently owns the worker lifecycle?
- How does shutdown happen today?
- Would runtime ownership make close-to-quiescence behavior more explicit or merely more complicated?
- Is there a targeted migration path that avoids broad architectural churn?","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when compaction and background worker ownership is mapped clearly, a migrate or defer decision exists with lifecycle-focused reasoning, and any recommended implementation scope is narrow and explicit.","notes":"Primary target area is src/compaction_worker.rs plus any directly coupled session-index or background ownership surfaces that materially affect runtime lifecycle reasoning.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:27.730343944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T04:31:49.329607161Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","blocking-io","compaction"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:46.331863193Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:27.730343944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","title":"Evaluate compaction and adjacent background workers for runtime ownership","description":"Evaluate compaction and adjacent background-worker ownership for possible runtime-managed execution. The key question is whether these workers currently live outside the runtime in a way that meaningfully weakens quiescent shutdown, cancellation composition, or lifecycle reasoning.","design":"Decision criteria:
- What currently owns the worker lifecycle?
- How does shutdown happen today?
- Would runtime ownership make close-to-quiescence behavior more explicit or merely more complicated?
- Is there a targeted migration path that avoids broad architectural churn?","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when compaction and background worker ownership is mapped clearly, a migrate or defer decision exists with lifecycle-focused reasoning, and any recommended implementation scope is narrow and explicit.","notes":"Primary target area is src/compaction_worker.rs plus any directly coupled session-index or background ownership surfaces that materially affect runtime lifecycle reasoning.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:27.730343944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:05:17.142598746Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T05:05:17.142573008Z","close_reason":"Rehosted background compaction onto runtime with abort-on-timeout and drop","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","blocking-io","compaction"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:46.331863193Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:27.730343944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.4.3","title":"Decide whether tool subprocess pipe pumps should remain dedicated threads","description":"Decide whether the subprocess stdout and stderr pump threads in the tools layer should remain dedicated raw threads or be partially absorbed into runtime-managed structure. This bead exists partly as a safeguard against over-eager cleanup: hard-blocking pipe readers are exactly the sort of place where a dedicated thread can still be the cleanest answer.","design":"Decision criteria:
- Does the current pipe-pump design introduce a real correctness, shutdown, or observability problem?
- Would a runtime-managed alternative genuinely improve semantics, or just move blocking complexity around?
- If the answer is to keep dedicated threads, record that decision explicitly and explain why.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when the subprocess pipe-pump design is evaluated explicitly, the bead records a keep or migrate decision with concrete failure-mode reasoning, and future contributors can tell whether the raw-thread choice is intentional.","notes":"Primary target area is the shell and bash tool path in src/tools.rs, and this bead is allowed to conclude that the current dedicated-thread approach is the right design.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:29.036846306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:46.656166661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","blocking-io","tools"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:46.656065332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:29.036846306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.4.4","title":"Implement approved worker migrations and preserve rationale for deferred thread islands","description":"Implement only the worker migrations that survive the three evaluation beads. This is intentionally downstream of explicit decisions so we do not create churn by converting worker islands merely because they look stylistically inconsistent.","design":"Implementation guidance:
- Pull in only the sites approved by BLOCKING_SESSION, BLOCKING_COMPACT, and BLOCKING_TOOLS.
- Preserve explicit rationale in code comments or bead notes where raw threads remain by design.
- Keep the patch set narrow and accompanied by targeted tests.","acceptance_criteria":"This bead is complete when only approved worker migrations land, deferred worker islands remain documented as intentional keep decisions, and changed lifecycle or ownership semantics are backed by tests.","notes":"This can close as an intentional no-op implementation bead if every evaluated worker island is deferred with explicit rationale instead of ambiguity.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:30.344035968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:47.654103274Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","blocking-io","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:30.344035968Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:46.983584483Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:47.318723104Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:47.654020249Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-xdcrh.5","title":"Extend deterministic Asupersync testing into core app and session surfaces","description":"Extend deterministic Asupersync-oriented testing into the core app and session surfaces touched by this effort. The extension system already demonstrates the right local standard for budget, deadline, and timer-driver-aware testing, and this workstream exists so context-propagation and lifecycle changes can be landed with evidence rather than confidence.","design":"Testing principles:
- Prefer deterministic timing and explicit budget or deadline assertions over wall-clock sleeps.
- Make each test prove a concrete semantic claim.
- Use the extension and runtime tests already present in the repo as the local pattern library, adapting them to app, session, or TUI surfaces as needed.","acceptance_criteria":"This workstream is complete when each implementation stream that lands code also lands focused regression coverage for budget inheritance, cancellation or checkpoint behavior, or worker-lifecycle semantics as applicable, and the new coverage is strong enough to keep future refactors honest.","notes":"This is part of the risk-reduction strategy that makes targeted Asupersync leverage acceptable in higher-level app code, not an abstract testing chore.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:31.637763201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:32.934033693Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","asupersync-leverage","lab-runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.5","depends_on_id":"bd-xdcrh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:31.637763201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":4007,"issue_id":"bd-xdcrh.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The extension runtime is already the local gold standard for budget, deadline, and timer-driver-aware testing. The point here is to bring that quality bar into app, session, and TUI surfaces where targeted Asupersync leverage changes would otherwise be too risky to land with confidence.","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:40:32Z"}]}
